content: Start a new game with the age-of-dragons-1024 seed

1024 CE. Late Spring. You are Lyndwell — a 22-year-old graduate of the Silver Conclave, fresh off the windswept plateau of the Silver Tower with a staff that channels but does not store. You came to the frontier to see the world, and the world has obliged: three days ago, the ancient red dragon Kargad descended on the town of Shallowstone in fire and thunder. The outer buildings are scorched. Half the population has fled. The other half is hiding in cellars or standing on the walls. Captain Bren of the town guard has 200 defenders and 100 able-bodied townsfolk who will fight. Across the central plains, the Lyondell Kingdom is mourning the loss of its crown prince to the same dragon. The Silver Conclave has sent no word. The Duskwood fey say nothing. The Black Iron Legion, as always, is waiting to be paid. The only spell you know that might matter is the one your thesis advisor warned you never to cast. End of the first scene. What do you do?
I want to introduce myself to Captain Bren, assess the town's defenses, and find out whether any of the townsfolk have useful information about the dragon's habits.

state.truncated_summary: faction_shallowstone_defenders.resources: {gold: 200, food: 800, iron: 150}. population: 12000 (half displaced). military: 55, stability: 50. province_dragonmount.controller_id: faction_dragonclaw_horde. character_kargad_red_wyrm.age: 842, location_id: province_dragonmount. character_captain_bren.loyalty: 95, location_id: province_shallowstone.

content: You find Captain Bren on the northern wall, kicking a half-buried arrow out of the parapet. He does not look at you when you approach, but he listens. 'A mage,' he says, finally. 'From the Tower?' When you tell him yes, his jaw tightens. 'Good. We need a mage. We need ten mages. We have one. The dragon came from the northeast, faster than any horse. Landed on the granary, ate three oxen, breathed fire across the smithy, then left. Three minutes. Didn't kill a single defender — wanted us scared, not dead. Scared people don't fight back. Then it took my wife.' A pause. 'Townsfolk are saying it took my wife because she's a tanner. Leather. Scaly. They think dragons like the smell.' He turns to face you, finally. 'I'm Bren. I don't trust mages. I trust competence. What can you do?'

I tell him what I can do: evocation up to 3rd level, abjuration up to 2nd, my capstone thesis on dragon-wake magical resonance. I propose a plan: I spend tonight abjuring the granary, the smithy, and the well — three points. Each ward lasts 8 hours and deflects one breath-weapon strike. We buy the town three more days. In exchange, I want his best scout to take me to within sight of Dragonmount, so I can take a magical reading of Kargad's presence. I have a hunch my thesis was about this exact moment.

event.reason: Lyndwell introduces herself to Captain Bren, offers a defensive plan, and requests resources for a research mission.

content: Bren looks at you for a long time. Then he nods. 'Wards on the granary, the smithy, and the well. Eight hours each. Done. You'll have Mara — she's our best scout, a half-elf, came in from the Duskwood last winter and never said why. She'll take you to within a day's march of Dragonmount, no closer. Two days' rations. And mage — if Mara comes back without you, the Conclave will hear about it. The archmage does not like her students dying in obscure ways.' The turn passes into evening. You spend the night weaving your wards. They hum, faintly, in the dark. By dawn, the granary glows a thin gold. The smithy follows an hour later. The well by noon. Bren watches in silence, and you see his shoulders drop — the first time he's relaxed in three days. Mara is waiting at the south gate. End of turn 0. Next: a march into dragon territory.
